$comment: azure-mi-ai-dba-demo — MCP server config EXAMPLE. Copy to mcp.config.json (git-ignored) and fill via env vars. NO secrets/connection strings hardcoded. Principle: READ-ONLY diagnostics; state changes require human approval via a separate path.
$mssql_primary_path: PRIMARY path for DB metadata/DMV is the official VS Code 'SQL Server (mssql)' extension in GitHub Copilot agent mode. The extension is itself the MCP tool host, so NO separate npm server is required: create an Azure SQL MI connection profile with Microsoft Entra auth and query DMVs/metadata read-only from agent mode. Official Microsoft SQL MCP Server (Data API Builder based, RBAC/telemetry): https://learn.microsoft.com/sql/mcp/ . The 'mssql' server below is a DISABLED standalone ALTERNATIVE (example only). NOTE: the previously referenced '@microsoft/mssql-mcp' npm package does NOT exist (npm 404) and has been removed.

mssql.$comment: OPTIONAL standalone MCP server — ALTERNATIVE to the VS Code mssql extension. EXAMPLE ONLY (community package, requires org security approval before use). Supports Microsoft Entra (aad) auth. Verified to exist on npm; DO NOT substitute a non-existent package name. Env var names are illustrative — confirm against the package's own docs. Never hardcode connection strings.

azure.$comment: Azure MCP (@azure/mcp — exists on npm, e.g. 3.0.0-beta.x) for Log Analytics, Defender for SQL, resource metadata. Auth via az login / DefaultAzureCredential (no secrets in config). Docs: https://github.com/Azure/azure-mcp . Usage: npx -y @azure/mcp

policy.notes: Diagnosis/inspection is read-only. Any DDL/DML (index create, plan guide, config change) is proposed as a script and applied only after explicit human approval.
